project: Royal Caribbean DAM — Split-Screen Detail View
overview: Replace the asset detail modal with a persistent split-screen panel. Clicking an asset card opens a detail panel on the right side of the screen (40% width). The grid and sidebar remain visible on the left (60%). The detail panel stacks content vertically: full-width image at top, then title and actions, then tags, then the collapsible metadata accordion below. The modal overlay is removed entirely.
goals: Clicking an asset opens a right-side detail panel instead of a modal overlay
The grid stays visible and interactive while the panel is open
Detail panel shows image at top (full-width), content stacked below
Panel closes via an X button or pressing Escape
Smooth slide-in animation when panel opens
nonGoals: No changes to the API or data fetching logic
No changes to the filter sidebar or search
No dual-panel (two assets open at once)
successMetrics: Clicking a card shows the detail panel on the right without any overlay or backdrop
The asset grid remains visible and scrollable while the panel is open
The image fills the full width of the panel at the top
Content (title, tags, metadata, CDN URL) is stacked vertically below the image
Clicking a different card in the grid updates the panel content
Panel closes on X click or Escape key
uiNotes: Layout when panel is closed: sidebar (240px) + main grid (flex:1) — unchanged from current
Layout when panel is open: sidebar (240px) + main grid (flex:1, min-width 0) + detail panel (400px, flex-shrink:0). The grid naturally shrinks to fill remaining space.

Detail panel: width 400px, height 100%, border-left '1px solid #E5E7EB', background #FFFFFF, overflow-y auto, flex-shrink 0. Slide in from right: transform translateX(400px) → translateX(0), transition '250ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)'.
Panel image zone: width 100%, aspect-ratio 16/9, object-fit cover, display block. Background #F3F4F6 while loading.
Panel content zone: padding 20px. Stacking order from top: (1) title + action buttons row, (2) Details accordion (collapsed by default), (3) CDN URL section, (4) Enrich button.

Details accordion contains: Enriched/Original pill toggle at top, then the key-value metadata rows, then a Tags section at the bottom showing tags as navy pill chips. Tags are ONLY inside the accordion — not shown outside it.

Tags row inside accordion: label 'Tags' (11px/600 uppercase muted), pills below. Only rendered when display_tags is non-empty. Tags must not appear both inside and outside the accordion — remove any always-visible tags summary that was added in prd-modal-ux.
Title: 16px/700, color #111827, flex:1. Action buttons (download, favourite, delete) in a row to the right — same icon-only 32px buttons as the current modal.

The detail panel inherits ALL content and logic from AssetDetailModal: enriched-first metadata, Enriched/Original pill toggle inside the accordion, variant strip, quality issues, enrich button, share button, admin-gated delete.
Variant strip: shown below the image if variants exist, same horizontal thumbnail strip design.
Close button: top-right of the panel, 32×32px, × symbol, color #9CA3AF.
When panel is open and user clicks a different card: content updates in place (no close/reopen animation — just data swap).
AssetDetailModal.jsx is removed and replaced by AssetDetailPanel.jsx. BrowsePage renders the panel inline instead of the modal.
The panel does NOT use position:fixed or z-index overlay — it is part of the normal document flow.
rules: Remove AssetDetailModal.jsx and its usage from BrowsePage.jsx
Create client/src/components/AssetDetailPanel.jsx with the same data-fetching logic (getAsset, getAssetVariants) and all the same content sections as the current modal
BrowsePage bodyRow layout: when selectedAssetId is set, render AssetDetailPanel as a sibling to the main grid div inside the bodyRow flex container
The bodyRow already has display:flex and overflow:hidden — the panel slots in naturally as a third flex child
AssetDetailPanel receives the same props as AssetDetailModal: selectedAssetId, onClose, isFavourited, onFavouriteToggle, adminMode

Panel slide-in: use a CSS transition on the panel's transform. When selectedAssetId changes from null to a value, render the panel and immediately transition from translateX(400px) to translateX(0). When closing, reverse the transition then remove from DOM (use a 'closing' state with 250ms timeout).
Escape key closes the panel — same as current modal
Clicking a card when panel is already open: just update selectedAssetId — panel stays open and re-fetches new asset
The share URL format is unchanged: window.location.origin + '/?asset=' + id

stories.title: AssetDetailPanel component — vertical layout with image at top
stories.description: As a content author, I want to see asset details in a side panel with the image at the top so I can evaluate the image and read metadata without losing context of the grid.
acceptanceCriteria: client/src/components/AssetDetailPanel.jsx is created
Panel container: width 400px, height 100%, border-left '1px solid #E5E7EB', background #FFFFFF, overflow-y auto, flex-shrink 0, position relative
Close button: position absolute, top 12px, right 12px, 32×32px, background none, border none, fontSize 20px, color #9CA3AF, cursor pointer, z-index 1
Image zone: width 100%, aspect-ratio 16/9, object-fit cover. Uses web_image_path or thumbnail_path via /api/assets/media/ route. Shows grey placeholder if no image.
Variant strip: rendered below the image when variants.length > 1 — horizontal scrollable strip of 64×64px thumbnails, same style as current modal
Content zone: padding 20px, display flex, flex-direction column, gap 16px

Title row: display flex, align-items flex-start, gap 8px. Title: 16px/700 color #111827, flex 1. Action buttons: '↓ Download' (text+icon, navy), favourite heart (♥/♡, toggles red when active), share (↗), and delete (🗑 — admin only). The favourite and delete buttons are icon-only 32×32px; Download and Share have text labels.
Tags row: always visible. 'Tags' label (11px/600 uppercase muted) above navy pill chips. Hidden if no tags.
Details accordion: same collapsible accordion as specified in prd-modal-ux US-004 — label 'Details', collapsed by default, contains Enriched/Original pill toggle and key-value metadata rows
CDN URL section: shown when asset.cdn_url exists — code block + copy button
Enrich button: shown when enrichment_source is null or pending
All data fetching logic copied from AssetDetailModal: getAsset on selectedAssetId change, getAssetVariants if variant_group_id set, enrichAsset, handleCopy, handleDelete, handleVariantClick
Escape key listener closes panel via onClose()
Example: clicking an asset card shows the panel with image at top and title below
Negative case: asset with no image shows grey placeholder in the image zone
npm run lint passes
npm run build passes

stories.title: BrowsePage — split-screen layout with slide-in panel
stories.description: As a content author, I want the detail panel to appear alongside the grid so I can keep browsing while reading asset details.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowsePage removes the AssetDetailModal import and renders AssetDetailPanel instead
AssetDetailPanel is rendered inside the bodyRow flex container as a direct sibling of the main content div, not as a fixed overlay
When selectedAssetId is null: panel is not rendered, grid takes full available width
When selectedAssetId is set: panel renders at 400px width, grid shrinks to fill remaining space (flex:1, min-width:0)
Panel slide-in animation: panel mounts with transform translateX(400px) then immediately transitions to translateX(0) over 250ms with cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1) easing
Panel slide-out: on close, transitions back to translateX(400px) over 200ms then unmounts (use a 'isClosing' state)
Clicking a different card while panel is open: selectedAssetId updates, panel stays open and shows new asset (no close/reopen)
AssetDetailModal.jsx can be deleted or kept but is no longer used in BrowsePage
The grid remains scrollable and cards remain clickable while the panel is open
Example: grid shows 4 columns with panel closed; with panel open grid shows ~2-3 columns depending on viewport
Example: clicking card A opens panel showing asset A; clicking card B updates panel to show asset B without animation
Negative case: pressing Escape while panel is open closes the panel
npm run lint passes
npm run build passes
